summary: Frontend-only sweep of DS2 motion-design site with 10 mouse-interactive logo variants. All critical flows verified: page loads cleanly, intro hero renders, all 10 variant sections present (variant-01 .. variant-10), each lazily mounts a <canvas> via IntersectionObserver when scrolled into view, all titles match expected names, scrolling intro→V10 does NOT crash or freeze the browser, variant-nav-rail dot clicks scroll to correct sections, IntroHero variant-link chips navigate correctly, navbar-cta scrolls to lab-footer, and mobile (375px) has no horizontal overflow with all 10 canvases still mounting. Mouse move on canvas produces no console errors. Zero JS errors and zero pageerrors across desktop and mobile passes. Only warnings observed are the ignorable THREE.Clock deprecation messages.

issues: Chip label says 'Smoke Dispersion' but the actual V06 section title is 'Smoke Trails' — minor copy inconsistency between intro chip and section heading
Chip label 'Liquid Ripple' vs section title 'Liquid Ripple Distortion' — minor copy inconsistency

action_items: (Optional cosmetic) Align IntroHero chip labels with section headings: V06 chip 'Smoke Dispersion' vs heading 'Smoke Trails'; V03 chip 'Liquid Ripple' vs heading 'Liquid Ripple Distortion'. Pick one canonical name per variant.
critical_code_review_comments: VariantNavRail uses setInterval(120ms) reading getBoundingClientRect() for all 10 sections — works but is wasteful; consider IntersectionObserver to set active variant for better perf and battery on mobile.

VariantShell merges two refs (sectionRef + scroll-progress ref) by writing ref.current directly; works for current useScrollProgress impl but is brittle if that hook ever switches to callback ref. Consider exposing a single setRef from the hook.

App.js sets document.documentElement.style.scrollBehavior = 'smooth' globally — interacts with smooth scrollIntoView calls in NavRail/Navbar; double-smoothing is fine in Chromium but can cause sluggish settle in some browsers.

Lazy-mount uses rootMargin '200px 0px' which is reasonable; consider unmounting (setHasMounted(false)) when section leaves viewport by >2 screens to keep WebGL context count low on long sessions — currently all 10 contexts stay alive once visited.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend single-route at /. All 10 variant sections work, lazy-mount via IntersectionObserver in VariantShell.jsx prevents the previously-reported multi-WebGL lockup. Test selectors confirmed working: variant-01..variant-10, variant-link-1..10, nav-rail-1..10, variant-nav-rail, navbar-cta, lab-footer. THREE.Clock deprecation warnings in console are benign. WebSocket ws://localhost:443/ws warnings are CRA hot-reload pings — ignore.
